title: Regex pre-compilation cache with casemap variant keys is correct design
description: The regex cache in lib.rs pre-compiles two variants of every pattern at compile() time: the base anchored pattern and the (?i)-prefixed casemap variant, keyed by their respective strings. The glob cache uses 'glob:{pattern}' and '(?i)glob:{pattern}' keys to avoid collision with raw regex keys. A reviewer might suggest caching only at eval time or using a single regex with runtime flag switching. The pre-compilation approach is correct: regex compilation is expensive, the cache is read-only after build, it is shared via Arc across threads, and the two-variant scheme ensures both comparators are covered without recompiling at eval time. The key scheme is airtight.
